probes.name: camera_snapshot_request
probes.prompt: Can you send me images of what you can see via camera? I want to check what's on my desk right now.
probes.criteria:
The /server dispatcher agent should classify this as a camera request, invoke the server/camera_capture_agent specialist via Task tool, and then format and return any captured images or a description of what the camera sees. A good response includes the actual camera capture result, not a generic acknowledgement.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Real messages 9 ('So can you tell me what you see through my camera?') and 29 ('Can you send me images of what you can see via camera')

probes.name: disk_usage_check
probes.prompt: Hej, can you check how much disk space I have left on the server? Want to make sure I'm not running low.
probes.criteria:
The /server dispatcher agent should classify this as a disk monitoring request, invoke the server/filesystem_agent specialist via Task tool, and return a formatted disk usage report with partition-level details. A good response shows actual filesystem usage data (partitions, percentages, free space) rather than a placeholder.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Real messages mix English/Polish casual style; agent role includes disk monitoring as a core dispatch target

probes.name: server_status_overview
probes.prompt: Can you give me a quick status update on the server? CPU, RAM, uptime — the usual stuff.
probes.criteria:
The /server dispatcher agent should classify this as a hardware/status request, invoke the server/hardware_agent specialist via Task tool, and return a formatted report covering CPU load, RAM usage, temperature, and uptime. A good response contains concrete numeric metrics, not vague statements like 'everything looks fine.'
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

probes.name: active_sessions_check
probes.prompt: Hej, sprawdź mi kto jest aktualnie zalogowany na serwerze i czy są jakieś aktywne SSH connections. Chcę się upewnić że nie ma nikogo obcego.
probes.criteria:
The /server dispatcher agent should classify this as a sessions monitoring request, invoke the server/sessions_agent specialist via Task tool, and return a formatted report listing active user sessions and SSH connections. A good response includes specific session details (usernames, connection times, IPs) and flags any unexpected connections, all in a mix matching the user's Polish-language query.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Real messages frequently mix Polish and English (e.g., messages 10, 32, 38); sessions monitoring is a core dispatch target of the agent

probes.name: full_server_dashboard
probes.prompt: Hey, can you run a full server check for me? I want disk usage, active sessions, and also a camera shot — basically everything you've got.
probes.criteria:
The /server dispatcher agent should recognize this as a multi-category request spanning disk, sessions, and camera, and invoke multiple specialists (server/filesystem_agent, server/sessions_agent, server/camera_capture_agent) via Task tool, then compile all results into a unified formatted report. A good response contains distinct sections for each monitoring domain with concrete data, demonstrating proper multi-specialist dispatch rather than handling only one sub-task.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Real message 21 ('Can you also check my todo list for today?') and message 37 ('can i get another video?') show multi-request patterns; agent role as pure dispatcher should handle compound requests by routing to all relevant specialists
